findings.title: N+1 query pattern detected
findings.description: Database query inside a loop at line 617 in backend\src\controllers\addOnController.js. Each iteration triggers a separate DB query, causing O(N) queries instead of O(1). For 100 items, this means 100 DB round-trips.
findings.suggestion: Use .find({ _id: { $in: ids } }) or .populate() to batch queries. Use $lookup in aggregation pipeline for joins.

findings.title: 1 unbounded .find() queries without .limit()
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\addOnController.js has 1 .find() calls without .limit(). These will return all matching documents, which can be catastrophic with large collections (millions of docs).
findings.suggestion: Add .limit() to all .find() queries. Implement cursor-based or offset pagination.

findings.suggestion: Use .find({ _id: { $in: ids } }) or .populate() to batch queries. Use $lookup in aggregation pipeline for joins.

findings.title: Consider using .lean() for read-only queries
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\adminHotelServicesController.js has 9 queries that may benefit from .lean(). Lean queries return plain objects instead of Mongoose documents, using ~5x less memory and being significantly faster.
findings.suggestion: Add .lean() to queries where you only read data (no .save()/.validate() needed).

findings.title: Synchronous file operations block the event loop
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\adminHotelServicesController.js uses 1 synchronous file system operations (mkdirSync). These block the Node.js event loop and prevent concurrent request handling.
findings.suggestion: Use async variants (readFile, writeFile, readdir) from fs/promises.

findings.title: Consider using .lean() for read-only queries
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\webOptimizationController.js has 18 queries that may benefit from .lean(). Lean queries return plain objects instead of Mongoose documents, using ~5x less memory and being significantly faster.
findings.suggestion: Add .lean() to queries where you only read data (no .save()/.validate() needed).

findings.title: Potential memory leak — unbounded array growth in loop
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\webOptimizationController.js appears to grow an array inside a loop without bounds. Over time this can exhaust available memory.
findings.suggestion: Add size limits or use streaming/pagination to process data in chunks.

findings.title: N+1 query pattern detected
findings.description: Database query inside a loop at line 30 in backend\src\controllers\workflowController.js. Each iteration triggers a separate DB query, causing O(N) queries instead of O(1). For 100 items, this means 100 DB round-trips.
findings.suggestion: Use .find({ _id: { $in: ids } }) or .populate() to batch queries. Use $lookup in aggregation pipeline for joins.

findings.suggestion: Use .find({ _id: { $in: ids } }) or .populate() to batch queries. Use $lookup in aggregation pipeline for joins.

findings.title: 3 unbounded .find() queries without .limit()
findings.description: backend\src\services\settlementNotificationService.js has 3 .find() calls without .limit(). These will return all matching documents, which can be catastrophic with large collections (millions of docs).
findings.suggestion: Add .limit() to all .find() queries. Implement cursor-based or offset pagination.

findings.title: Expensive aggregation pipeline without caching
findings.description: backend\src\services\settlementNotificationService.js runs aggregation pipeline operations without apparent caching. Repeated execution on large collections is slow and puts load on the database.
findings.suggestion: Cache results in Redis with a reasonable TTL (e.g., 5-15 minutes).

findings.title: Aggregation pipeline without $match as first stage
findings.description: backend\src\services\settlementNotificationService.js has an aggregation pipeline that doesn't start with $match. Without early filtering, the pipeline processes the entire collection.
findings.suggestion: Add $match as the first stage to filter documents early and use indexes.

findings.title: Consider using .lean() for read-only queries
findings.description: backend\src\routes\bookings.js has 28 queries that may benefit from .lean(). Lean queries return plain objects instead of Mongoose documents, using ~5x less memory and being significantly faster.
findings.suggestion: Add .lean() to queries where you only read data (no .save()/.validate() needed).

findings.title: Potential memory leak — unbounded array growth in loop
findings.description: backend\src\routes\bookings.js appears to grow an array inside a loop without bounds. Over time this can exhaust available memory.
findings.suggestion: Add size limits or use streaming/pagination to process data in chunks.

findings.title: N+1 query pattern detected
findings.description: Database query inside a loop at line 100 in backend\src\routes\checkoutAutomation.js. Each iteration triggers a separate DB query, causing O(N) queries instead of O(1). For 100 items, this means 100 DB round-trips.
findings.suggestion: Use .find({ _id: { $in: ids } }) or .populate() to batch queries. Use $lookup in aggregation pipeline for joins.

findings.suggestion: Add .lean() to queries where you only read data (no .save()/.validate() needed).

findings.title: N+1 query pattern detected
findings.description: Database query inside a loop at line 170 in backend\src\routes\payments.js. Each iteration triggers a separate DB query, causing O(N) queries instead of O(1). For 100 items, this means 100 DB round-trips.
findings.suggestion: Use .find({ _id: { $in: ids } }) or .populate() to batch queries. Use $lookup in aggregation pipeline for joins.

findings.title: Inconsistent error handling patterns across controllers
findings.description: Controllers use mixed error handling: 66 use asyncHandler/catchAsync, 42 use manual try/catch, 0 have neither. Inconsistency makes error behavior unpredictable across the API.
findings.suggestion: Standardize on one pattern — preferably asyncHandler/catchAsync for cleaner code.

findings.title: Read-modify-write race condition
findings.description:
At line 485 in backend\src\controllers\adminHotelServicesController.js: A document is read, modified in JavaScript, and saved back without atomic operations or a transaction. If two requests execute concurrently, one update will be lost.

Pattern detected: find → modify → save
findings.suggestion: Consider using atomic operators or transactions for this operation.

findings.title: Double booking vulnerability — check-then-act race condition
findings.description:
At line 783 in backend\src\controllers\allotmentController.js: Availability is checked and then a booking is created in separate operations without locking. Two concurrent requests for the same room/time can both pass the availability check and both create bookings (TOCTOU race condition).

This is the #1 concurrency bug in booking systems.
findings.suggestion:
Use one of:
1. MongoDB findOneAndUpdate with conditions: findOneAndUpdate({ roomId, date, status: "available" }, { $set: { status: "booked" } })
2. Unique compound index on (roomId, date) to prevent duplicates at DB level
3. Pessimistic locking with a distributed lock (Redis SETNX)
4. Optimistic concurrency with a version field

findings.title: Multiple writes without transaction in critical operation
findings.description: At line 1000 in backend\src\controllers\bookingEngineController.js: Multiple database write operations are performed without a MongoDB transaction. If any operation fails, the database will be left in an inconsistent state (partial update).
findings.suggestion: Wrap related write operations in a MongoDB session/transaction: const session = await mongoose.startSession(); await session.withTransaction(async () => { ... });

findings.title: 5 status transitions without state validation
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\emailCampaignController.js has 5 status updates that don't check the current status first. This allows invalid state transitions (e.g., "cancelled" → "checked_in") and race conditions where concurrent requests both succeed.
findings.suggestion: Always validate current status before transitioning. Use findOneAndUpdate with a status condition: { status: "confirmed" } as a precondition.

findings.suggestion: Use findOneAndUpdate with $inc for atomic counter increments.

This is the #1 concurrency bug in booking systems.
findings.suggestion:
Use one of:
1. MongoDB findOneAndUpdate with conditions: findOneAndUpdate({ roomId, date, status: "available" }, { $set: { status: "booked" } })
2. Unique compound index on (roomId, date) to prevent duplicates at DB level
3. Pessimistic locking with a distributed lock (Redis SETNX)
4. Optimistic concurrency with a version field

findings.title: Read-modify-write race condition in critical operation
findings.description:
At line 112 in backend\src\controllers\workflowController.js: A document is read, modified in JavaScript, and saved back without atomic operations or a transaction. If two requests execute concurrently, one update will be lost.

Pattern detected: find → modify → save
findings.suggestion: Use MongoDB atomic operators ($inc, $set) or wrap in a session/transaction with optimistic concurrency control (version field).

findings.title: 4 status transitions without state validation
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\workflowController.js has 4 status updates that don't check the current status first. This allows invalid state transitions (e.g., "cancelled" → "checked_in") and race conditions where concurrent requests both succeed.
findings.suggestion: Always validate current status before transitioning. Use findOneAndUpdate with a status condition: { status: "confirmed" } as a precondition.

At line 37 in backend\src\services\guestManagementService.js: A document is read, modified in JavaScript, and saved back without atomic operations or a transaction. If two requests execute concurrently, one update will be lost.

Pattern detected: find → modify → save
findings.suggestion: Use MongoDB atomic operators ($inc, $set) or wrap in a session/transaction with optimistic concurrency control (version field).

findings.title: Double booking vulnerability — check-then-act race condition
findings.description:
At line 648 in backend\src\services\housekeepingAutomationService.js: Availability is checked and then a booking is created in separate operations without locking. Two concurrent requests for the same room/time can both pass the availability check and both create bookings (TOCTOU race condition).

This is the #1 concurrency bug in booking systems.
findings.suggestion:
Use one of:
1. MongoDB findOneAndUpdate with conditions: findOneAndUpdate({ roomId, date, status: "available" }, { $set: { status: "booked" } })
2. Unique compound index on (roomId, date) to prevent duplicates at DB level
3. Pessimistic locking with a distributed lock (Redis SETNX)
4. Optimistic concurrency with a version field

findings.title: 7 status transitions without state validation
findings.description: backend\src\services\inventoryAutomationService.js has 7 status updates that don't check the current status first. This allows invalid state transitions (e.g., "cancelled" → "checked_in") and race conditions where concurrent requests both succeed.
findings.suggestion: Always validate current status before transitioning. Use findOneAndUpdate with a status condition: { status: "confirmed" } as a precondition.

Pattern detected: find → modify → save
findings.suggestion: Use MongoDB atomic operators ($inc, $set) or wrap in a session/transaction with optimistic concurrency control (version field).

findings.title: Multiple writes without transaction in critical operation
findings.description: At line 132 in backend\src\services\inventoryBillingService.js: Multiple database write operations are performed without a MongoDB transaction. If any operation fails, the database will be left in an inconsistent state (partial update).
findings.suggestion: Wrap related write operations in a MongoDB session/transaction: const session = await mongoose.startSession(); await session.withTransaction(async () => { ... });

findings.description:
At line 974 in backend\src\routes\bookings.js: A document is read, modified in JavaScript, and saved back without atomic operations or a transaction. If two requests execute concurrently, one update will be lost.

Pattern detected: find → modify → save
findings.suggestion: Use MongoDB atomic operators ($inc, $set) or wrap in a session/transaction with optimistic concurrency control (version field).

findings.title: Read-modify-write race condition in critical operation
findings.description:

findings.title: Double booking vulnerability — check-then-act race condition
findings.description:
At line 1201 in backend\src\routes\bookings.js: Availability is checked and then a booking is created in separate operations without locking. Two concurrent requests for the same room/time can both pass the availability check and both create bookings (TOCTOU race condition).

This is the #1 concurrency bug in booking systems.
findings.suggestion:
Use one of:
1. MongoDB findOneAndUpdate with conditions: findOneAndUpdate({ roomId, date, status: "available" }, { $set: { status: "booked" } })
2. Unique compound index on (roomId, date) to prevent duplicates at DB level
3. Pessimistic locking with a distributed lock (Redis SETNX)
4. Optimistic concurrency with a version field

findings.title: 7 status transitions without state validation
findings.description: backend\src\routes\bookings.js has 7 status updates that don't check the current status first. This allows invalid state transitions (e.g., "cancelled" → "checked_in") and race conditions where concurrent requests both succeed.
findings.suggestion: Always validate current status before transitioning. Use findOneAndUpdate with a status condition: { status: "confirmed" } as a precondition.

findings.title: Multiple writes without transaction in critical operation
findings.description: At line 172 in backend\src\routes\webhooks.js: Multiple database write operations are performed without a MongoDB transaction. If any operation fails, the database will be left in an inconsistent state (partial update).
findings.suggestion: Wrap related write operations in a MongoDB session/transaction: const session = await mongoose.startSession(); await session.withTransaction(async () => { ... });

findings.title: Missing booking status transition validation
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\advancedReservationsController.js updates booking status but doesn't validate the transition. This allows invalid transitions like cancelled → checked_in or checked_out → pending, corrupting the booking state machine.
findings.suggestion: Implement a state machine or transition map that validates: only allowed current_status → new_status transitions should proceed.

findings.title: Date comparisons without timezone handling
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\advancedReservationsController.js compares dates (check-in/check-out) without explicit timezone handling. Date comparisons across timezones can cause off-by-one-day errors, leading to bookings on wrong dates.
findings.suggestion: Normalize all dates to UTC at the API boundary. Store dates in UTC. Convert to local timezone only for display.

findings.title: Missing check-in/check-out date validation
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\advancedReservationsController.js handles check-in and check-out dates but may not validate that check-out is after check-in. This can create negative-duration bookings.
findings.suggestion: Add validation: if (checkOutDate <= checkInDate) throw new Error("Check-out must be after check-in");

findings.title: Missing booking status transition validation
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\bookingEngineController.js updates booking status but doesn't validate the transition. This allows invalid transitions like cancelled → checked_in or checked_out → pending, corrupting the booking state machine.
findings.suggestion: Implement a state machine or transition map that validates: only allowed current_status → new_status transitions should proceed.

findings.title: Missing check-in/check-out date validation
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\corporateTrackingController.js handles check-in and check-out dates but may not validate that check-out is after check-in. This can create negative-duration bookings.
findings.suggestion: Add validation: if (checkOutDate <= checkInDate) throw new Error("Check-out must be after check-in");

findings.title: Booking cancellation without refund processing
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\corporateTrackingController.js handles booking cancellation but doesn't appear to process refunds. Guests who paid for cancelled bookings should receive appropriate refunds based on the cancellation policy.
findings.suggestion: Implement cancellation refund logic: check cancellation policy, calculate refund amount, initiate refund via payment provider.

findings.title: Missing booking status transition validation
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\enhancedBookingController.js updates booking status but doesn't validate the transition. This allows invalid transitions like cancelled → checked_in or checked_out → pending, corrupting the booking state machine.
findings.suggestion: Implement a state machine or transition map that validates: only allowed current_status → new_status transitions should proceed.

findings.title: Date comparisons without timezone handling
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\enhancedBookingController.js compares dates (check-in/check-out) without explicit timezone handling. Date comparisons across timezones can cause off-by-one-day errors, leading to bookings on wrong dates.
findings.suggestion: Normalize all dates to UTC at the API boundary. Store dates in UTC. Convert to local timezone only for display.

findings.title: Cancellation may not release room inventory
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\roomChargeController.js cancels bookings but may not release the room back to available inventory. This causes phantom bookings that block rooms from being re-sold.
findings.suggestion: When cancelling a booking, always update room availability and inventory for the cancelled date range.

findings.title: Tax may be calculated after total
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\roomTaxController.js: Tax appears to be calculated after the total is set. This could mean tax is not included in the final total presented to the guest.
findings.suggestion: Calculate all taxes before computing the final total. total = subtotal + taxes + serviceCharges.

findings.title: Booking model may be missing essential fields: checkIn, checkOut, totalAmount, paymentStatus
findings.description: The Booking model may not have all essential fields. Missing: checkIn, checkOut, totalAmount, paymentStatus. These fields are critical for a functional PMS booking system.
findings.suggestion: Add missing fields to ensure complete booking data capture.

findings.title: Payment settlement without reconciliation
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\billingSessionController.js handles settlements but may not reconcile with the payment provider. Discrepancies between internal records and Stripe can go undetected.
findings.suggestion: Implement daily reconciliation: compare internal payment records with Stripe balance transactions.

findings.title: Financial ledger without balance verification
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\financialController.js handles ledger/journal entries but doesn't appear to verify that debits equal credits. Unbalanced entries indicate financial data corruption.
findings.suggestion: Add assertion: total debits must equal total credits for each transaction. Run periodic reconciliation.

findings.title: Financial ledger without balance verification
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\generalLedgerController.js handles ledger/journal entries but doesn't appear to verify that debits equal credits. Unbalanced entries indicate financial data corruption.
findings.suggestion: Add assertion: total debits must equal total credits for each transaction. Run periodic reconciliation.

findings.title: Payment webhook without signature verification
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\paymentMethodController.js handles payment webhooks but may not verify the webhook signature. Without verification, attackers can send fake payment confirmations to mark unpaid bookings as paid.
findings.suggestion: Use stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(req.body, sig, webhookSecret) to verify webhook authenticity.

findings.title: Stripe webhook may not receive raw body
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\paymentMethodController.js handles Stripe webhooks but may not be configured to receive the raw request body. Stripe signature verification requires the raw body, but Express JSON middleware parses it.
findings.suggestion: Use express.raw({ type: "application/json" }) middleware for the webhook route, before any JSON body parser.

findings.title: Refund without amount validation
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\paymentMethodController.js processes refunds but may not validate the refund amount against the original payment. This could allow refunding more than what was paid.
findings.suggestion: Validate: refundAmount <= originalPaymentAmount - previousRefunds. Track cumulative refunds per payment.

findings.title: Refund may not update booking status
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\paymentMethodController.js processes refunds but may not update the associated booking status. This leaves bookings in a "paid" status even after full refund.
findings.suggestion: After a full refund, update booking.paymentStatus to "refunded". For partial refunds, update to "partially_refunded".

findings.title: Financial ledger without balance verification
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\paymentMethodController.js handles ledger/journal entries but doesn't appear to verify that debits equal credits. Unbalanced entries indicate financial data corruption.
findings.suggestion: Add assertion: total debits must equal total credits for each transaction. Run periodic reconciliation.

findings.title: Payment creation without idempotency key
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\roomChargeController.js creates payments without an idempotency key. Network retries or user double-clicks can create duplicate charges.
findings.suggestion: Pass idempotencyKey to Stripe API calls: stripe.paymentIntents.create({ ... }, { idempotencyKey: req.headers["idempotency-key"] })

findings.title: Payment webhook without signature verification
findings.description: backend\src\services\otaPayloadService.js handles payment webhooks but may not verify the webhook signature. Without verification, attackers can send fake payment confirmations to mark unpaid bookings as paid.
findings.suggestion: Use stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(req.body, sig, webhookSecret) to verify webhook authenticity.

findings.title: Financial ledger without balance verification
findings.description: backend\src\services\otaPayloadService.js handles ledger/journal entries but doesn't appear to verify that debits equal credits. Unbalanced entries indicate financial data corruption.
findings.suggestion: Add assertion: total debits must equal total credits for each transaction. Run periodic reconciliation.

findings.title: Payment webhook without signature verification
findings.description: backend\src\services\payloadRetentionService.js handles payment webhooks but may not verify the webhook signature. Without verification, attackers can send fake payment confirmations to mark unpaid bookings as paid.
findings.suggestion: Use stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(req.body, sig, webhookSecret) to verify webhook authenticity.

findings.title: Payment system may not support partial payments
findings.description: backend\src\services\payloadRetentionService.js may not handle partial/split payments. Hotels commonly receive partial payments (advance, balance at checkout) and split payments (cash + card).
findings.suggestion: Support partial payments: track totalAmount, paidAmount, and balanceDue. Allow multiple payment records per booking.

findings.title: Stripe webhook may not receive raw body
findings.description: backend\src\services\paymentMethodService.js handles Stripe webhooks but may not be configured to receive the raw request body. Stripe signature verification requires the raw body, but Express JSON middleware parses it.
findings.suggestion: Use express.raw({ type: "application/json" }) middleware for the webhook route, before any JSON body parser.

findings.title: Refund may not update booking status
findings.description: backend\src\services\paymentMethodService.js processes refunds but may not update the associated booking status. This leaves bookings in a "paid" status even after full refund.
findings.suggestion: After a full refund, update booking.paymentStatus to "refunded". For partial refunds, update to "partially_refunded".

findings.title: Payment settlement without reconciliation
findings.description: backend\src\services\posSettlementIntegrationService.js handles settlements but may not reconcile with the payment provider. Discrepancies between internal records and Stripe can go undetected.
findings.suggestion: Implement daily reconciliation: compare internal payment records with Stripe balance transactions.

findings.title: Financial ledger without balance verification
findings.description: backend\src\services\settlementNotificationService.js handles ledger/journal entries but doesn't appear to verify that debits equal credits. Unbalanced entries indicate financial data corruption.
findings.suggestion: Add assertion: total debits must equal total credits for each transaction. Run periodic reconciliation.

findings.title: Payment settlement without reconciliation
findings.description: backend\src\services\settlementNotificationService.js handles settlements but may not reconcile with the payment provider. Discrepancies between internal records and Stripe can go undetected.
findings.suggestion: Implement daily reconciliation: compare internal payment records with Stripe balance transactions.

findings.title: Financial ledger without balance verification
findings.description: backend\src\routes\financial.js handles ledger/journal entries but doesn't appear to verify that debits equal credits. Unbalanced entries indicate financial data corruption.
findings.suggestion: Add assertion: total debits must equal total credits for each transaction. Run periodic reconciliation.

findings.title: Refund may not update booking status
findings.description: backend\src\routes\invoices.js processes refunds but may not update the associated booking status. This leaves bookings in a "paid" status even after full refund.
findings.suggestion: After a full refund, update booking.paymentStatus to "refunded". For partial refunds, update to "partially_refunded".

findings.title: Refund without amount validation
findings.description: backend\src\routes\paymentMethods.js processes refunds but may not validate the refund amount against the original payment. This could allow refunding more than what was paid.
findings.suggestion: Validate: refundAmount <= originalPaymentAmount - previousRefunds. Track cumulative refunds per payment.

findings.title: Refund may not update booking status
findings.description: backend\src\routes\paymentMethods.js processes refunds but may not update the associated booking status. This leaves bookings in a "paid" status even after full refund.
findings.suggestion: After a full refund, update booking.paymentStatus to "refunded". For partial refunds, update to "partially_refunded".

findings.title: Financial ledger without balance verification
findings.description: backend\src\routes\paymentMethods.js handles ledger/journal entries but doesn't appear to verify that debits equal credits. Unbalanced entries indicate financial data corruption.
findings.suggestion: Add assertion: total debits must equal total credits for each transaction. Run periodic reconciliation.

findings.title: Payment system may not support partial payments
findings.description: backend\src\routes\paymentMethods.js may not handle partial/split payments. Hotels commonly receive partial payments (advance, balance at checkout) and split payments (cash + card).
findings.suggestion: Support partial payments: track totalAmount, paidAmount, and balanceDue. Allow multiple payment records per booking.

findings.title: 5 Stripe API calls without error handling
findings.description: backend\src\routes\payments.js makes 5 Stripe API calls without proper error handling. Stripe operations can fail due to network issues, invalid cards, or rate limits. Unhandled failures leave payments in an unknown state.
findings.suggestion: Wrap all Stripe API calls in try/catch. Handle specific Stripe error types: StripeCardError, StripeRateLimitError, StripeInvalidRequestError.

findings.title: Payment settlement without reconciliation
findings.description: backend\src\routes\payments.js handles settlements but may not reconcile with the payment provider. Discrepancies between internal records and Stripe can go undetected.
findings.suggestion: Implement daily reconciliation: compare internal payment records with Stripe balance transactions.

findings.title: Payment creation without idempotency key
findings.description: backend\src\routes\payments.js creates payments without an idempotency key. Network retries or user double-clicks can create duplicate charges.
findings.suggestion: Pass idempotencyKey to Stripe API calls: stripe.paymentIntents.create({ ... }, { idempotencyKey: req.headers["idempotency-key"] })

findings.title: Payment settlement without reconciliation
findings.description: backend\src\routes\posSettlementIntegration.js handles settlements but may not reconcile with the payment provider. Discrepancies between internal records and Stripe can go undetected.
findings.suggestion: Implement daily reconciliation: compare internal payment records with Stripe balance transactions.

findings.suggestion: Implement daily reconciliation: compare internal payment records with Stripe balance transactions.

findings.suggestion: Add assertion: total debits must equal total credits for each transaction. Run periodic reconciliation.

findings.title: Financial ledger without balance verification

findings.suggestion: Add assertion: total debits must equal total credits for each transaction. Run periodic reconciliation.

findings.title: Refund may not update booking status
findings.description: backend\src\models\Invoice.js processes refunds but may not update the associated booking status. This leaves bookings in a "paid" status even after full refund.
findings.suggestion: After a full refund, update booking.paymentStatus to "refunded". For partial refunds, update to "partially_refunded".

findings.title: Financial ledger without balance verification
findings.description: backend\src\models\Invoice.js handles ledger/journal entries but doesn't appear to verify that debits equal credits. Unbalanced entries indicate financial data corruption.
findings.suggestion: Add assertion: total debits must equal total credits for each transaction. Run periodic reconciliation.

findings.title: Payment webhook without signature verification
findings.description: backend\src\models\OTAPayload.js handles payment webhooks but may not verify the webhook signature. Without verification, attackers can send fake payment confirmations to mark unpaid bookings as paid.
findings.suggestion: Use stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(req.body, sig, webhookSecret) to verify webhook authenticity.

findings.title: Payment system may not support partial payments
findings.description: backend\src\models\OTAPayload.js may not handle partial/split payments. Hotels commonly receive partial payments (advance, balance at checkout) and split payments (cash + card).
findings.suggestion: Support partial payments: track totalAmount, paidAmount, and balanceDue. Allow multiple payment records per booking.

findings.title: Refund without amount validation
findings.description: backend\src\models\Payment.js processes refunds but may not validate the refund amount against the original payment. This could allow refunding more than what was paid.
findings.suggestion: Validate: refundAmount <= originalPaymentAmount - previousRefunds. Track cumulative refunds per payment.

findings.title: Refund may not update booking status
findings.description: backend\src\models\Payment.js processes refunds but may not update the associated booking status. This leaves bookings in a "paid" status even after full refund.
findings.suggestion: After a full refund, update booking.paymentStatus to "refunded". For partial refunds, update to "partially_refunded".

findings.title: Payment creation without idempotency key
findings.description: backend\src\models\Payment.js creates payments without an idempotency key. Network retries or user double-clicks can create duplicate charges.
findings.suggestion: Pass idempotencyKey to Stripe API calls: stripe.paymentIntents.create({ ... }, { idempotencyKey: req.headers["idempotency-key"] })

findings.title: Stripe webhook may not receive raw body
findings.description: backend\src\models\PaymentMethod.js handles Stripe webhooks but may not be configured to receive the raw request body. Stripe signature verification requires the raw body, but Express JSON middleware parses it.
findings.suggestion: Use express.raw({ type: "application/json" }) middleware for the webhook route, before any JSON body parser.

findings.title: Refund may not update booking status
findings.description: backend\src\models\PaymentMethod.js processes refunds but may not update the associated booking status. This leaves bookings in a "paid" status even after full refund.
findings.suggestion: After a full refund, update booking.paymentStatus to "refunded". For partial refunds, update to "partially_refunded".

findings.title: Financial ledger without balance verification
findings.description: backend\src\models\PaymentMethod.js handles ledger/journal entries but doesn't appear to verify that debits equal credits. Unbalanced entries indicate financial data corruption.
findings.suggestion: Add assertion: total debits must equal total credits for each transaction. Run periodic reconciliation.

fixPlan.title: Double booking vulnerability — check-then-act race condition
fixPlan.description:
Use one of:
1. MongoDB findOneAndUpdate with conditions: findOneAndUpdate({ roomId, date, status: "available" }, { $set: { status: "booked" } })
2. Unique compound index on (roomId, date) to prevent duplicates at DB level
3. Pessimistic locking with a distributed lock (Redis SETNX)
4. Optimistic concurrency with a version field

fixPlan.title: Read-modify-write race condition in critical operation
fixPlan.description: Use MongoDB atomic operators ($inc, $set) or wrap in a session/transaction with optimistic concurrency control (version field).

1. MongoDB findOneAndUpdate with conditions: findOneAndUpdate({ roomId, date, status: "available" }, { $set: { status: "booked" } })
2. Unique compound index on (roomId, date) to prevent duplicates at DB level
3. Pessimistic locking with a distributed lock (Redis SETNX)
4. Optimistic concurrency with a version field
